lesson.menu_desc: Documents, responsibilities and resources with have and has -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 6 -- Who Has What? Talking About Documents with Have & Has
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Carlos Eduardo | Aula 6 -- Who Has What? (Have & Has)

vocab.title: Match each document word to its meaning
defs: an official paper or digital text with information
a table of rows and columns for numbers (like Excel)
an official paper that proves something is true
permission to open or use a system
one form of a document; version 2 is newer than version 1
a set of information saved with one name on a computer
a big organized store of data on a computer
a program or thing you use to do a job

paras: I have many documents at Sucafina. I have access to the IMS, the internal management system. I have six documents to produce each cycle: the property checklist, the farm member spreadsheet, and the EUDR analysis. Each producer has a file in the database.

Not every farm has all the documents. Fazenda Sao Joao has an Impact certificate, but it doesn't have a corrective action plan. Fazenda Boa Vista has the plan, but it doesn't have IMS access yet. I check who has what, and I report it to my manager.

The verb have shows possession. I have, you have, we have, they have. He, she, and it use has: the farm has, the producer has. For no, we use don't have and doesn't have. Be careful: use have for documents and resources, and to be (am, is, are) for descriptions. We say 'I have six documents', not 'I am six documents'.
reading.source: Alumni by Better -- A1 reading, have & has for documents

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Carlos explains how to grow coffee on a farm.
Carlos talks about which documents and resources he and the farms have, using have and has.
Carlos writes an email to the Rwanda office.

items: Carlos has access to the IMS.
The text says: 'I have access to the IMS.'
Fazenda Sao Joao has a corrective action plan.
The text says it has an Impact certificate but 'doesn't have a corrective action plan.'
The text says: 'He, she, and it use has: the farm has, the producer has.'
We use have for documents: 'I have six documents', not 'I am six documents'.

parts: access to the IMS. The farm
a property checklist. Fazenda Sao Joao
a corrective action plan. Does the producer

gapfill.text: Use have with I, you, we, they. Use has with he, she, it (the farm has, the producer has). For no, use don't have (I/you/we/they) and doesn't have (he/she/it).

bank.label: Useful sentences with have and has
items: I have access to the IMS.
The farm has a property checklist.
It doesn't have a corrective action plan.
Do you have the updated spreadsheet?
Does the producer have all the documents?
We have eighty-two criteria to verify.
Each farm has a file in the database.
I have six documents to produce each cycle.

lf.title: Have or Be? Choose the right verb

lf.text: Use have / has for things you possess (documents, access, a file). Use to be (am, is, are) for descriptions (Swiss, international, responsible).

items.situation: A farm has the Impact certificate but no corrective action plan. Say both facts.
tips: It doesn't have a corrective action plan.

items.situation: Ask the producer if he has all the required documents.
tips: Do you have all the documents?
Does the producer have the file?

items.situation: Say two things you have at work.
tips: I have access to the IMS.
I have six documents to produce each cycle.

items.situation: The warehouse has no updated checklist. Report it.
tips: The warehouse doesn't have the updated checklist.
It doesn't have the property checklist.

items: Look at the producer table. Say what one farm has and doesn't have, using has and doesn't have.
Ask about a second farm: 'Does Fazenda Boa Vista have IMS access?'
Describe three farms with no table, from memory. Use has and doesn't have.
The auditor asks: 'Do you have all eighty-two criteria verified?' Answer honestly with have or don't have.

answerkey.title: Reveal the producer status
list: Fazenda Sao Joao has an Impact certificate. It doesn't have a corrective action plan.
Fazenda Boa Vista has a corrective action plan. It doesn't have IMS access.
Fazenda Cachoeira has IMS access and the property checklist. It has all the documents.
answerkey.note: Have / has = possession. Use has with he/she/it (the farm), and doesn't have for the negative.

listenings.text: Let me tell you what I have at Sucafina. I have access to the IMS, our internal management system. I have six documents to produce each cycle. Every producer has a file in the database, and each farm has a property checklist. Some farms have an Impact certificate, but not every farm has a corrective action plan. I check who has what, and I report it to my manager. That is my job with the documents.

Hi Carlos, this is Emma with a quick note about compliance. For the audit, every farm needs the right documents. If a farm doesn't have a corrective action plan, we cannot approve it. If a producer doesn't have IMS access, please help him. Remember: use have and has for the documents, and doesn't have for the ones that are missing. Send me the list of who has what before the deadline. Thank you, and good luck.

extra_audio.text: First, Carlos opens the database. Every producer has a file. He checks who has the documents. Fazenda Sao Joao has an Impact certificate. But it doesn't have a corrective action plan. Carlos reports who has what to his manager.
